element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Choose from 4 golfers with varying range and control tradeoffs
Straightforward fairway layouts teach aiming and distance control
Abundant bunkers on US course force precision aiming
Japan course emphasizes water obstacles and tight boundaries

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based golf rhythm with moment-to-moment tension during shot setup and execution. US course emphasizes bunker avoidance (rapid local hazards), Japan course emphasizes layout mastery (strategic tension).

difficulty_curve.early_game: US Course: Straightforward fairways introduce basic mechanics, then bunkers increase precision demands
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Course variety forces adaptation: bunker-heavy US course contrasts with water/boundary-focused Japan course
difficulty_curve.late_game: Japan course final holes combine water hazards with tight boundaries, demanding integrated mastery of aiming and control
notable_spikes: Transition from fairway play to bunker-dense layouts
Introduction of water hazards in Japan course

notable_level_patterns.description: Four playable golfers with range/control tradeoffs (Pretty Amy: easy control/limited range; Miracle Chosuke/Super Mex: balanced; Big Jumbo: best range/worst control). Players select character matching skill level or course demands.
Two courses with distinct hazard profiles: US course emphasizes bunkers and rough ground; Japan course emphasizes water and tight boundaries. Escalates mechanical and spatial mastery demands.
Courses range from relatively straightforward fairways to elaborate hazard arrangements. Spatial complexity increases alongside hazard variety.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: PGA West Stadium Course (US course model)
